vitals.terrain: Hilly with multiple climbs and descents through vineyards and olive groves

course_description.overview: The 117 km long course starts at Piazza del Duomo in Pistoia, heads south through Monte San Baronto with four back-to-back climbs, passes Vinci (birthplace of Leonardo da Vinci), climbs to Montespertoli in Chianti hills, features the Castro climb to 420m, a gradual 12 km climb to San Baronto village, and finishes with a descent and flat stretch[1][4].

key_segments.name: Monte San Baronto climbs
key_segments.description: Four back-to-back climbs gaining/dropping 200m each after initial flat 21 km[1]

key_segments.name: Montespertoli climb
key_segments.description: Climb in Chianti hills with views of Florence[1]

key_segments.name: Castro climb
key_segments.description: Leads to race high point at 420m, followed by twisty descent to Vinci[1]

key_segments.name: San Baronto village climb
key_segments.description: Gradual climb rarely steeper than 3% through vineyards and olive groves to 365m[1]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging with 2000m elevation over 117 km, featuring pro-level climbs in cycling heartland, back-to-back ascents, and scenic but demanding Tuscan terrain[1][4].

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Pisa (PSA), Florence (FLR), Rome FCO, or Milan MXP; car rentals available at airports; Pistoia near Florence, Lucca, Pisa (within 1 hour drive)[1]
logistics.accommodation: Pistoia offers medieval charm with hotels; nearby Montecatini Terme for spas; explore Florence, Lucca, Pisa[1]
logistics.registration_details: Includes jersey, timing chip, goodie bag, finisher medal, full support; register via official GFNY site[1][6]
logistics.bike_logistics: Packet pickup at finish 'La Cattedrale'; Made in Italy GFNY jersey mandatory[1][6]
logistics.local_considerations: Tuscan cuisine like ribollita; explore medieval streets, vineyards; cycling paradise with pro heritage[1]

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic Tuscan granfondo through pro cycling heartland and Leonardo's birthplace.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking authentic Italian granfondo with climbs, history, and scenery.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the notable cycling paradise and cultural immersion.
pro_tips: Train for repeated 200m climbs on narrow roads.
Stay in Pistoia for medieval vibe and easy access.
